title: Include Subitems in Progress Column in Parent Item
excerpt: I’d love to see the ability for subitems to be included in the progress tracking column of the parent item. For example, if I’m breaking down a piece of marketing campaign into subitems or tasks, it would be really helpf…

posts.author: meghankmc (Meghan McMaster)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love to see the ability for subitems to be included in the progress tracking column of the parent item. For example, if I’m breaking down a piece of marketing campaign into subitems or tasks, it would be really helpful to see the status of the subitems apply in the parent items progress tracking.

Or if there’s a similar function that already exists, please let me know!

image2576×744 79.5 KB

posts.author: CHRfreeIST (Jai Coward)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. The Status Column of the sub items needs to be reflected in the parents progress bar. This will give a true total progress of the parent item as one works their way through the sub items.

posts.author: Paul.Hutchison (Paul Hutchison)
posts.cookedHtml:
there is an automation that helps for completion, but does not measure progress

image1264×286 65.8 KB

posts.author: badinoff (Boris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Surprised to see how little votes this has. This was the first thing I tried when learning about Progress Tracking column. Would love to see an update from Monday since this has been out there since 2022.

A few other thoughts:

  1. If a tracked column does not have a value (e.g. no status set) it should be calculated as 0%.
  2. Gantt chart should reflect the progress of each item via progress bar color corresponding to the calculated value in the Progress Tracking field.
  3. Nice to have: a Progress Tracking for groups based on parent items progress calculation.
  4. Report/widget for Progress Tracking
